missing_key_points: Did not explicitly mention CDW as a competing trivial/correlated interpretation, despite the rubric requiring CDW/trivial/artifact/inhomogeneity concepts.
Did not explicitly use PL and RMCD together for the nu=-1/3 signature; it mentions PL and RMCD only in general context.
Some cited claim IDs, especially the excluded node and thermodynamic/AFM paper references, are not fully visible in the supplied context excerpt, so citation support is partly unverifiable.
summary: Strong cautious answer. It correctly abstains from overinterpreting the specific nu=-1/3 optical claim, separates observation/inference/uncertainty, and lists plausible artifacts and separating evidence such as Hall/Streda response, incompressibility, filling dependence, spatial maps, and excitonic controls. Main weakness is missing explicit CDW/trivial interpretations and relying on a couple of citations not clearly present in the supplied context.
